subject: [COMMON] Fix shell scripts missing shebangs
commit_message:
[COMMON] Fix shell scripts missing shebangs

pointed out by checkbashisms.

$ tox -e checkbashisms |grep 'interpreter line' | cut -d' ' -f2
  |xargs grep -lv '#!/bin/sh' | xargs sed -i -e '1i#!/bin/sh' -e '1i\\'

plus manual fixes

Issue-ID: OOM-2643
Signed-off-by: Guillaume Lambert <guillaume.lambert@orange.com>
Change-Id: Ic41fec6ebadd162cecf889f2b119ac82551bd21d

Patch Set 6:

any reason to put a blank line after shebang?

Patch Set 6:

> Patch Set 6:
> 
> any reason to put a blank line after shebang?

It's the most common and historical convention to clearly state the end of the shebangs.
First UNIX shebangs were limited to 16 chars.
Some system still limits the first line shebang length
and you have to use alternatives such as sbang and long-shebang in the second line
to pass options.

https://github.com/spack/sbang
https://github.com/shlevy/long-shebang
